test_cases.name: [SynchronizedAfterSuite]
[SynchronizedBeforeSuite]

ai_summary.summary: Test failed to download cluster-api v1.12.8 manifest due to GitHub 502 error during setup phase

ai_analysis.root_cause: The error occurred during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to fetch the cluster-api v1.12.8 core-components.yaml from GitHub. The request failed with HTTP 502 Bad Gateway: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502" (build-log.txt line 1348). This is a transient infrastructure issue affecting GitHub's release endpoint. The clone-records.json confirms the repository was successfully cloned but does not include the v1.12.8 tag, indicating the error occurred during the release download phase rather than local repository state. No controller manager logs were available (capz-system namespace empty) as the setup phase failed before controller deployment.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the job as this is a transient infrastructure error. If the issue persists, verify the existence of the v1.12.8 release in kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api and check for any ongoing GitHub outages. The error occurred before controller deployment, so no cluster-specific artifacts were generated.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed due to missing/invalid Calico manifest during clusterctl repository creation

ai_analysis.root_cause: The build log shows a failure at line 1341 when clusterctl attempted to read the ClusterResourceSet manifest from `/home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/templates/addons/calico.yaml`. The error `"failed to get component source YAML from URL"` indicates the file was either missing, inaccessible, or invalid. This blocked repository creation, causing the SynchronizedBeforeSuite to fail before cluster creation started. The artifacts/clusters directory is empty, confirming no clusters were created.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: artifacts show the Calico manifest path was inaccessible but do not confirm whether the file is missing, invalid, or incompatible. Would need to check the CAPZ repository's `templates/addons/calico.yaml` content and version compatibility with cluster-api v1.12.8.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed due to invalid Cluster API version v1.12.8 in test configuration, causing a 502 Bad Gateway error during core-components.yaml download.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test attempted to fetch core-components.yaml for Cluster API v1.12.8 from GitHub releases (line 1348 in build-log.txt). The version v1.12.8 does not exist in the Cluster API releases (as of 2026-05-31), causing a 502 error. No clusters or controllers were created (confirmed via empty discover_clusters and discover_controllers outputs). This is a configuration error in the test suite, not a transient infrastructure issue.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Update the test configuration to use a valid Cluster API version (e.g., v1.13.2) that exists in the GitHub releases. Modify the version reference in test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go and associated test setup files.

ai_summary.summary: GitHub release download failure during clusterctl setup

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failed to fetch core-components.yaml from GitHub release URL 'https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml' with status code 502. This occurred during clusterctl repository initialization in SynchronizedBeforeSuite at line 1346 of build-log.txt. The local repository only contains v1.11.11 artifacts (artifacts/repository/cluster-api/v1.11.11), but the test requires v1.12.8 which failed to download.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the job as this is a temporary GitHub outage. If the issue persists, update the test configuration in test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go to use Cluster API v1.11.11 (available in repository) by modifying the KUBERNETES_VERSION_UPGRADE_TO variable to match the available version.
